commit.message:
M5: Implement Purge/BAN broadcast proxy with ACL and rate limiting

Adds internal/proxy/ package implementing a broadcast HTTP proxy that
receives PURGE, BAN, and xkey invalidation requests and fans them out
in parallel to all Varnish pods of the target VinylCache cluster.

Also fixes pre-existing build errors in internal/controller/ (missing
agentPort, finalizerName constants).

fix: round per-phase cost values to two decimal places in metrics comment (#111)

The metrics PR comment displayed raw floating-point costs (e.g. $1.2961435000000001)
for per-phase rows. The total row already rounded via awk, but individual phase rows
did not. Apply printf '%.2f' rounding in _format_phase_row to match.

Co-authored-by: alex-claude <alex@alexs-Mac-mini.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Overhaul AI difficulty — much more aggressive

- AI_TICK reduced from 20s to 6s (normal), 3s (hard), 12s (easy)
- AI now takes up to 2 build actions per tick instead of 1
- Labs: always build when affordable, no random gate
- Silo: built as soon as possible, no random gate
- Reactors: 80% chance (was 55%), cap raised 3→5
- Oil fields: 65% chance (was 45%), cap raised 3→5
- Jammers: 60% chance (was 40%), cap raised 4→6
- Operations run every tick alongside building
- SWEEP: 40% (was 22%), STEAL: 55% (was 35%), RECON: 50% (was 30%)
- SABOTAGE: 88% when target found (was 75%)
- AI now also targets revealed reactors with SABOTAGE priority
- Early game: AI gets a reactor at 12s head-start

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: correct PostgreSQL minimum version from 9.5+ to 13+ (#53)

The ordered outbox transport uses pg_snapshot_xmin(), pg_current_xact_id(),
and xid8 — all introduced in PostgreSQL 13. The documentation incorrectly
stated 9.5+ (which only covers FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED). Also adds
PostgreSQL 13+ to README requirements alongside MySQL/MariaDB.

Fixes #49

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions (#8)

Two workflows: CI (lint/test/build on PRs and pushes to main) and
Deploy (auto-deploy to Fly.io after CI passes on main). Requires
FLY_API_TOKEN secret in GitHub repo settings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix set values getting overwritten due to WU row index mismatch

The auto-save in toggleSet and finishWorkout mapped DOM rows to sets
by index, but hidden WU rows caused a mismatch — S1's DOM values
would be read into WU2's slot, shifting everything. Also removed the
old WU sync code that copied WU1 values to all other WU sets (no
longer needed since all WU rows are now visible). Added localStorage
persistence to updateSet so weight/rep changes save immediately.

- AppConfig: new labs_path field loaded from LABS_PATH env var
- menu.py: "My Labs" option appears when LABS_PATH is set; reuses _launch_scenario
- .env.example: document LABS_PATH setting
- requirements.txt: add pyyaml for YAML topology parsing
- Scanner finds 52 real labs (40 CCNP + 12 CCIE) from the Layer8 workbooks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(engine): persist trend_aggregate accumulation across invocations, add partition_by to all examples

Three fixes for trend_aggregate and playground examples:

1. TrendAggregate accumulated state: Events were lost between execute_op() calls
   because the accumulated vector was a local variable recreated each invocation.
   Moved it to TrendAggregateConfig so it persists across calls — sum, avg, and
   count_events now compute correctly over the full event history.

2. CountEvents type resolution: HashMap iteration order could map a type index
   back to an alias (e.g., "readings") instead of the real event type name
   (e.g., "sensor_reading"), causing count to always be 0. Fixed with a two-pass
   builder that sets aliases first, then overwrites with real event type names.

3. Playground examples: Added .partition_by(field) to all sequence pattern examples
   (brute-force, ddos, error-storm, kleene-aggregates, first-last, distinct-count,
   rate-detection) and restored multi-entity test data to demonstrate correct
   per-entity isolation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: function overlap GO term format mismatch, white 3D viewer bg, remove broken compare toggle

- Fix function overlap always being 0: DB stores GO terms as
  "GO:0005576:C:extracellular region" but InterPro returns "GO:0008152".
  Now normalize DB terms to just "GO:XXXXXXX" before Jaccard comparison.
- Change 3D viewer background from dark (#0f172a) to white.
- Remove the "Compare with toxin" toggle which was causing errors due
  to unreliable AlphaFold DB availability for venom toxins.
- Rewrite protein_3d.py to use inline 3Dmol.js HTML embed instead of
  py3Dmol._make_html() which fails in Streamlit's sandboxed iframe.

commit.message:
fix(security): WS first-message auth + body size limit — closes #58 #46 (#64)

* fix(security): move WebSocket auth to first-message, add body size limit — closes #58 closes #46

- WebSocket /stream endpoint now accepts auth via first message instead of
  URL query param (CWE-598), closing with 4001 on timeout/failure
- Frontend sends {"type":"auth","token":"..."} on ws.onopen
- MaxBodySizeMiddleware rejects Content-Length > 64KB with HTTP 413

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: backend unit tests for body size limit and WS auth hardening

- TestBodySizeLimit: normal/oversized/exact-limit request tests
- TestWebSocketAuth: verifies token param removed from endpoint signature
- Playwright: asserts WebSocket URL contains no token= query param

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(security): address PR #64 review — WS disconnect handling, Content-Length validation, keepalive, reconnect guard

- Handle WebSocketDisconnect explicitly to avoid closing already-dead sockets
- Validate Content-Length header to prevent ValueError on malformed values
- Add keepalive ping with 30s timeout to detect dead WS connections
- Skip reconnect on 4001 auth failure to prevent infinite loops
- Assert wsUrls.length > 0 in Playwright test to prevent vacuous pass
- Replace inspect.getsource anti-pattern with behavioral WS auth tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(lint): resolve all ruff errors — UP041, F401, I001, F841 — CI green

* chore: gitignore Playwright test-results artifacts

---------

consistency, and extracts applyNewPrompt helper to deduplicate the
two user-prompt code paths.

* Revert "Add copy button to collapsed 'Create this table' toggle header"

This reverts commit 82b573738341f5c5e1154c6067746842e0a1d0f0.

* Fix invisible copy buttons on sample-tables page

The .copy-btn class had opacity:0 set globally (for SyntaxBlock hover reveal),
making all SampleTableCode copy buttons invisible since they weren't inside a
.syntax-block. Fix: use new .code-copy-btn class with SVG clipboard/check icons
matching the SyntaxBlock style, positioned absolutely in .sample-code-pre-wrapper.

Also updates CopyCodeBlock to use the same icon approach.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Vitest test suite with pre-push hook and GitHub Actions CI

- Install vitest, @vitest/coverage-v8, vite-tsconfig-paths, simple-git-hooks
- Add vitest.config.ts with tsconfigPaths for @/ alias resolution
- Export parseExamples/parseRemarks from route.ts for testability; fix description regex to skip leading blank line
- Add 4 test files: content-integrity, mdx, search, parsers (40 tests total)
- Add .github/workflows/test.yml for CI on push/PR to main
- Add 15 missing function pages discovered by content-integrity tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add 330 missing function pages to achieve full spec coverage

Audited all pages against the official Microsoft Power Query M function
reference and created pages for every missing function. Total coverage
grows from 177 to 507 function pages.

Categories added:
- List: 50 new pages (List.Reverse, List.Zip, List.Union, Date/Time
  sequence generators, set operations, statistics, etc.)
- Table: 55 new pages (Table.Column, Table.Transpose, Table.FuzzyJoin,
  Table.SplitColumn, membership/ordering/row-operation functions, etc.)
- Text: 23 new pages (Text.Start/End/Middle, delimiters, padding, etc.)
- Character, Guid, Lines, Splitter, Combiner, Comparer, URI: 15 new pages
- Date: 45 new pages (all Date.IsIn* filter functions, week/quarter/day
  helpers, format/parse functions)
- DateTime: 20 new pages (DateTime.IsIn* functions, AddZone, FromText, etc.)
- DateTimeZone: 11 new pages
- Duration: 5 new pages
- Time: 4 new pages
- Number: 42 new pages (trig, bitwise, type conversions, log/exp, etc.)
- Record: 9 new pages
- Type: 18 new pages
- Value: 9 new pages
- Accessing Data: 24 new pages (Sql, SharePoint, Folder, OData, PDF,

perf: optimize Core Web Vitals - split idols chunk, defer scripts, fix CLS

- Split idols.ts (11MB) into separate lazy-loaded chunk via manualChunks
- Split i18n data into separate chunk (194KB → 55KB gzip)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add admin endpoints to trigger zoning scrape and permit scrape on demand

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ws): send auth confirmation so client exits Connecting state (#82)

* fix(cd): use kubectl set image instead of rollout restart

rollout restart recycles pods but starts them with whatever image is
already in the deployment spec — it does NOT pull the newly-built image.
Set image explicitly to SHORT_SHA before waiting for rollout so every
service code change actually deploys the new container image.

Also add REGISTRY env var to the deploy-to-production job (it was only
defined in build-and-push, making it unavailable to the deploy steps).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ws): unique MQTT client ID per pod for TelemetryStreamManager

Both UI replicas used hardcoded client_id="pulse-stream-manager".
EMQX enforces unique client IDs — Pod B's connect kicks Pod A, Pod A
reconnects and kicks Pod B, creating an infinite 2-second reconnect
loop that kept the dashboard in "Connecting" state.

Fix: append socket.gethostname() to the client ID so each pod has a
unique ID and they coexist without kicking each other.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ws): send auth confirmation so client exits Connecting state

Deadlock: after auth the server entered receive_json() loop waiting for
subscribe commands; the client waited for the first server message before
sending subscribe commands. Neither moved first -> UI showed "Connecting"
forever.

Fix: send {"type":"connected"} immediately after auth succeeds. The client
treats any first message as auth confirmation, sets _authConfirmed=true,
and sends its pending subscribe commands. The server then responds with
"subscribed" acks and live data flows normally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Add course verification: 8 scripts covering lectures 7-22

Systematic computational verification of the GR lecture course using
TensorGR.jl. Each script verifies every nontrivial calculation from
the corresponding lectures — no println cheating.

Scripts and what they verify:
- lec07: CovD axioms, Christoffel formula, metric compatibility (5 checks)
- lec09_10: Riemann commutator, symmetries, Bianchi, Einstein, geodesic deviation (8 checks)
- lec12: Lie derivative, Killing equation, bracket identities (5 checks)
- lec13: Einstein equations, trace, dust conservation, linearised inverse (11 checks)
- lec14: Linearised Riemann/Ricci, gauge transformation, trace reversal (11 checks)
- lec17: FLRW Christoffel, Ricci, scalar, Einstein, Friedmann (symbolic components)
- lec19: Schwarzschild Christoffel, Ricci=0, K=48M²/r⁶ (15 checks, symbolic)
- lec21_22: Effective potential, ISCO at r=6M, photon sphere at r=3M (11 checks)

Filed issues for gaps: TGR-dhp (TOV solver), TGR-61p (geodesic ODE),
TGR-293h (symmetry reduction ansatz).

Fix: restore Symbolics as weakdep in Project.toml.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(decoder): comprehensive x86-64 null-deref recovery decoder

Extend decode_x86_load() to cover the full one-byte opcode map and
common two-byte (0F xx) opcodes that touch memory, eliminating the
need for per-crash patch cycles when ld-linux hits a null page.

One-byte additions:
- 0x00/01/08/09/10/11/18/19/20/21/28/29/30/31: OP r/m,r (mem dest)
- 0xC6/C7: MOV r/m, imm8 / imm16/32
- 0xC0/C1: shift/rotate r/m, imm8 (Group 2)
- 0xD0-0xD3: shift/rotate r/m by 1 or CL
- 0x69/6B: IMUL reg, r/m, imm32/imm8
- 0xF6/F7: Group 3 TEST/NOT/NEG/MUL/DIV (with correct immediate sizing)
- 0xFE/FF: Group 4/5 INC/DEC/CALL/JMP/PUSH r/m

Two-byte (0F) additions:
- 0xAF: IMUL reg, r/m
- 0xA3/AB/B3/BB: BT/BTS/BTR/BTC r/m, r
- 0xBA: Group 8 BT* r/m, imm8
- 0x90-0x9F: SETcc r/m8
- 0xC0/C1: XADD r/m, r
- 0x10-0x17/0x28-0x2F: SSE scalar/packed moves

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(test): update Verify snapshots for THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.TXT (#1054)

## Summary
- Update Verify snapshot baselines to include `THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.TXT`
added by PR #1053
- This file was added to the NuGet package via csproj but the test
baselines were not updated
- All open PRs are currently blocked by this regression

## Root Cause
PR #1053 added `THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.TXT` to the package contents. The
test workflow only runs on PRs (not push to main), so the snapshot
mismatch was not caught before merge.

## Test plan
- [ ] PackageTests.Baseline passes locally
- [ ] CI test matrix passes (ubuntu-arm, windows)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

improved transparency regarding included third-party dependencies.

Nuevo módulo scrapper/runner_paralelo.py que ejecuta el scraper en
múltiples pestañas en paralelo con timeout de 10s por página, loop
continuo con pausa configurable y backup automático de resultados.

- ejecutar_scraper.py: CLI con opciones --ciclos, --urls y --pausa
- scrapper/runner_paralelo.py: lógica de ejecución paralela y backup
- COMO_INICIAR_SCRAPER.md: guía paso a paso para iniciar el scraper
- config_candidatos.json: timeout reducido a 10s, URLs de Registraduría,
  carpeta backup y pausa entre ciclos

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
